_meta.description: Fonds locaux d'investissement (FLI) — borough-level micro-loans for Montreal businesses. Each Montreal borough administers its own FLI through a CDEC or local development organization.

program_name_en: Fonds locaux d'investissement (FLI) — Local Investment Fund
plain_language_summary: Every Montreal borough has a Fonds local d'investissement (FLI) — a pool of money specifically for local businesses. These are micro-loans typically ranging from $5,000 to $50,000, administered by local economic development organizations (CDECs, PME MTL centres). Because they're local and less publicized than national programs, they're often less competitive and faster to access.

amount.note: Varies by borough — some go up to $100,000 for the right project. Verify with your local administrator.

interest_rate: Below market — typically 4-6% fixed (verify with your local FLI)

location.detail: Business must be located in the specific borough whose FLI you're applying to
location.note: Each borough has its own FLI — you apply to your specific borough's fund

immigration_status.note: FLIs are locally administered and often more flexible on immigration status than federal programs — verify with your local administrator

local_economic_impact.detail: Preference for businesses that create local jobs, serve the local community, or operate in priority sectors for that borough
examples: Home daycare creates local childcare (priority in many boroughs)

criteria.business_plan: Required — PME MTL advisors can help prepare

borough_administrators_montreal.administrator: CDEC Rosemont–Petite-Patrie or local PME MTL Grand Nord
RESO (Regroupement économique et social du Sud-Ouest)
CDEC Ahuntsic-Cartierville or PME MTL Grand Nord

what_you_need: Proof of Montreal address (personal and business)

timeline.note: Local funds often move faster than national programs

why_this_is_a_hidden_gem: Less known than Futurpreneur or PME MTL — less competition
Locally administered means more personal relationship with your advisor
More flexible on immigration status in many boroughs
Can be stacked with other programs (Futurpreneur + FLI + PME MTL)
Local administrators understand your specific neighbourhood's business environment
stacking_note: FLI loans can often be combined with PME MTL financing and other programs. A business in Rosemont could potentially access: FLI (up to $50K) + PME MTL Young Entrepreneurs Fund ($20K) + Futurpreneur ($75K if 18-39) for substantial startup capital.
common_mistakes: Not knowing your borough's FLI exists — most entrepreneurs don't
Going to the wrong administrator — each borough has a different organization running its FLI
Not asking PME MTL advisors about the FLI — they know which borough funds have money available
Applying too late in the fiscal year — some FLIs run out of funds before year end
application_url: Contact your borough's PME MTL office or CDEC — see borough administrators above
government_body: Administered locally through CDECs and PME MTL, funded by Ville de Montréal
